name: ADR for deferred-mechanics decisions
description: When a parent ADR commits to a removal/migration target but defers the mechanics, follow-up ADR scope is: pin the version, deletion sequence (atomic vs staged), post-removal CLI behavior, user-data orphan handling, doc scrub. Reuse parent ADR's no-callers verification rather than re-doing it. ADR-0052 follows ADR-0039 with this pattern.

content: ADR amendments use 'amends: ADR-NNNN' frontmatter to follow through on a parent commitment without superseding it. Pattern observed across ADR-0046/0048/0050/0051/0052. Distinct from supersession (which replaces). When proposal-date and accepted-date differ, include both 'date:' and 'accepted:' lines (ADR-0048 shape). Additionally: ADR bodies should reference section names (§Section) over line numbers — section anchors survive future edits while line numbers rot.
